field_values.action_summary: The Hatter reveals his watch, which tracks the day but not the hour. He explains that because he quarreled with "Time," it is perpetually tea-time. This explanation establishes the surrealist rules governing their constant movement around the table.
field_values.emotional_beat: From intellectual debate to a sense of temporal absurdity.
field_values.camera_intent: Extreme close-up of the watch mechanism/face; Medium shots of group reactions to the concept of Time
field_values.end_state: scene resolves into the next narrative beat
movement_notes: Physical handling of the watch must remain consistent between hands and table surfaces
Lighting must reflect a constant, unchanging tea-time state despite temporal shifts
field_values.transition_notes:
# Scene Contract: CH007_SC002

## Production Intent
Establish the surreal temporal rules of the tea party through a physical object reveal. The scene must ground the abstract concept of Time into a tangible, mechanical conflict between characters.

## Required Cast
- Alice (alice)
- The Hatter (the_hatter)
- The March Hare (march_hare)
- The Dormouse (dormouse)

## Required Environments
- Under the tree at the tea table (tea_party_table_setting)

## Continuity Constraints
- Physical handling of the watch must remain consistent between hands and table surfaces.
- Lighting/time-of-day consistency: The scene represents a static time state.

## Beat List
- BT001: The Hatter reveals his watch to the group.
- BT002: The logic of Time is explained via the watch face.
field_values.dialogue_relevance: dialogue supports scene intent and continuity
